sovereign_credit_rating.value: S&P: A-, Moody's: A2, Fitch: A

resource_self_sufficiency_index.methodology: Average of min(production/consumption, 1.0) for energy, food, water, minerals

political_risk_premium_bps.methodology: Sovereign rating implied spread + adjustments for instability, conflict, sanctions, autocracy

market_accessibility_score.methodology: Average of capital_account_openness, stock_market_cap/200, financial_dev_index, ease_of_business/100

narrative.executive_summary: Poland's defense spending reached $38B reflecting its frontline NATO position near Russia and Ukraine. EU defense consensus supports further increases. GDP growth at 3.5% is among the strongest in Europe. Political gridlock between Tusk government and PiS president continues. Oil price surge creates moderate import cost pressure.
key_changes_this_week: Defense spending at $38B -- among highest in NATO as share of GDP
No major domestic events beyond ongoing political gridlock
narrative.outlook: Poland's defense-led fiscal expansion continues given proximity to the Russia-Ukraine war. Economic growth remains robust. Political gridlock limits reform agenda but defense spending has bipartisan support.
narrative.investor_implications: Polish defense sector benefits from sustained spending. PLN faces modest pressure from risk-off. WIG20 faces mixed signals. Polish government bonds carry higher yield than eurozone peers. EU structural funds continue to support infrastructure.
narrative.data_quality_note: Poland macro data is high quality from GUS and NBP.

active_alerts.title: Poland sees record defense spending $38B but president-PM institutional conflict threatens EU funds
active_alerts.description: Poland's defense spending at $38B with strong_growth trend in military expenditure. However, PM Tusk and President Nawrocki locked in institutional tug-of-war. Labour law reform rollback threatens EUR 2.5B in EU Recovery Fund. Despite political friction, Poland expected to record highest GDP growth in EU.
